target.name: Stark Defence
target.founder: Florian Seibel, Uwe Horstmann, Johannes Schaback

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Stark Defence is a Series A defense and security company founded by Florian Seibel, Uwe Horstmann, and Johannes Schaback, currently carrying a €3.5B (~$4.0B) valuation on $100M+ total raised. The peer universe comprises 8 private-stage comparables in Defense & Security, alongside 2 public references (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). The private peer set includes companies at Series B stage such as Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B).

Two valuation methods apply. Stage-Progression uses the $100M raised proxy with a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implying $200M. Peer-Median analysis across the 8-company comparable set yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range of $125M to $3.0B. This peer cohort skews toward Series B stage, introducing upward pressure relative to Stark's current stage.

Triangulation across both methods produces a valuation range of $170M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $600M. The current €3.5B mark sits substantially above the high end of this analysis. Key uncertainties include the timing and milestones required to justify Series B-level pricing, the applicability of later-stage comparables to early-stage performance, and sector-specific risk factors in Defense & Security procurement cycles.
